people.text: Leo wants a quiet café where he can study for two hours. He needs good Wi-Fi and does not want loud music.
Marta is meeting a friend with a baby. They need space for a pushchair and somewhere that welcomes young children.
Sam wants lunch near the station before catching a train. He only has twenty minutes and needs hot food quickly.
Hannah wants to buy fresh cakes for a birthday party. She needs to order them in advance and collect them early.
Ravi prefers cafés with outdoor tables. He wants a cheap breakfast before work on weekdays.

texts.text: The Reading Room has [6]silent tables upstairs[/6], reliable [6]free Wi-Fi[/6] and no background music before 5 p.m. Customers often bring laptops or textbooks and stay for long study sessions.
Little Spoon Café has wide spaces between tables and staff happily make room for [7]pushchairs[/7]. There is a children’s menu, high chairs and a small box of toys for younger visitors.
Platform Bites is opposite the railway station and serves soup, toasties and pasta boxes in under ten minutes. It is ideal for customers who need [8]hot food quickly before a train[/8].
Bella Bakery makes fresh cakes for celebrations. Customers can [9]order birthday cakes two days ahead[/9] and [9]collect them from 7.30 a.m.[/9] before the shop becomes busy.
Morning Yard opens at 7 a.m. from Monday to Friday. It has [10]outdoor tables[/10] and offers a [10]low-cost breakfast deal[/10] with toast, fruit and coffee.
Jazz Cup is popular in the evenings because local musicians play live music most nights.
The Long Table serves large family meals on Sundays but does not open before midday.
Sweet Box sells chocolates and biscuits, but it does not take cake orders.

answers.explanation: Leo needs quiet study space and Wi-Fi.
Marta needs a child-friendly café with pushchair space.
Sam needs fast hot food near the station.
Hannah needs advance cake orders and early collection.
Ravi wants outdoor tables and cheap weekday breakfast.
